commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"`, recursive match composition, blocked-send suppression metadata, reply-routing context propagation, tests, docs, and a Vitest CI heap default.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review finding by source inspection: PR head durable delivery reads `params.session?.inboundPeer`, while the gateway `message.send` path still builds session context without that field. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: A concrete repair is possible, but missing real behavior proof plus public security/config API direction make this a maintainer-review item before any automation lane.

target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and the inbound peer for the current turn.
- Propagates inbound peer context through reply routing and durable outbound delivery.
- Reports blocked sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes instead of transport failures.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs for the new send-policy predicate.

Fixes #85175.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/channels/message/send.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: sendPolicy can now deny outbound delivery when the outbound target does not match the inbound peer for the current turn.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, branch `fix/send-policy-peer-equals-inbound-peer`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts`; `pnpm test src/channels/message/send.test.ts`; `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts`; `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: send-policy tests cover `peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` allow, mismatch denial, missing inbound peer behavior, multiple inbound peer candidates, and `allOf` composition. Durable message send tests cover `denied_by_send_policy` suppression propagation. `check:changed` passed core typecheck, core test typecheck, lint, runtime sidecar loader guard, import cycle guard, and related changed-file checks.

Observed result after fix: focused tests passed and `pnpm check:changed` completed with 0 lint warnings, 0 lint errors, and 0 runtime import cycles.

What was not tested: no live provider send was performed against Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, or Discord.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
